From the retrieved articles, there is consistent mention across several references indicating that cleft lip and palate exhibit a multifactorial inheritance pattern involving both genetic predisposition and environmental influences. Multiple sources indicate that while certain genetic syndromes might cause cleft lip and/or palate, the vast majority of cases arise from complex interactions between genetic factors and non-genetic elements, supporting the concept of multifactorial inheritance rather than being purely monogenic or polygenic.
\boxed{C} Based on the consistency in the evidence pointing towards a multifactorial model combining both genetic predispositions and environmental factors influencing the development of cleft lip and palate, I conclude that option C (Multifactorial) correctly describes its mode of inheritance.
```markdown

\boxed{C} Based on the consistency in the evidence pointing towards a multifactorial model combining both genetic predispositions and environmental factors influencing the development of cleft lip and palate, I conclude that option C (Multifactorial) correctly describes its mode of inheritance.
